DESCRIPTION: Test sending a CREATE operation message with different statuses via GPD message API
NAME: A CREATE message with status PARTIALLY_PAID

DESCRIPTION:
Send the same CREATE VALID message twice.

Because send_gpd_message derives the Idempotency-Key deterministically from
the message id (msg_id), reusing the same payload reuses the same key.

Expectations:
- Both calls return HTTP 200 (idempotent, not an error).
- The resourceId in both responses is identical, proving the API returned
  the cached response and did not process the message twice.
- Querying by notice number returns exactly one RTP, confirming that only
  one record was written to the database.
NAME: Two CREATE VALID messages with the same Idempotency-Key both succeed but only one RTP is stored
All auth fixtures expose a valid Bearer token format

DESCRIPTION:
Parametrized contract test generated from the Activation API OpenAPI spec.

Schemathesis derives one test case per endpoint defined in the spec, generating
request data (path params, query params, body) that is valid according to the schema.
Each case is executed against the live API and the response is validated with
CONTRACT_CHECKS.
NAME: test_activation_contract[GET /activations/payer]
Non-integer size returns 400
A debtor is activated and retrieved by activation id
An RTP sent when EPC response contains an unknown field is ignored and remains in status SENT
An RTP is sent to Poste service with activated fiscal code
Find by payer id request must contain lower case fiscal code
Sending a GPD message with null id returns 400

DESCRIPTION:
Test RFC callback DS12N with non-existing service provider, cancel via REST Web API.

Flow:
1. Activate payer
2. Send an RTP via GPD message (CREATE VALID)
3. Cancel the RTP via REST cancel endpoint with cancel_reason → RTP to RFC_SENT
4. Send DS12N callback with non-existing BIC (MOCKSP99)
5. Verify callback is rejected with 400 (service provider not found)
NAME: Failed RFC callback for non existing Service Provider - DS-12N RJCR compliant - through Web API cancel
An RTP is sent through CBI API
RTP is successfully cancelled with reason MODT
